reasoning: The informal statement asserts that the specific collection of all rational open boxes is a basis for ℝ². The CORRECTED formalization captures this by explicitly constructing the set via Set.image2 over all Ioo intervals with rational endpoints. The ORIGINAL instead quantifies over an arbitrary set S with only the hypothesis that every element of S is some rational box — crucially missing the converse requirement that S contains all rational boxes. This makes the original a stronger (universally quantified) claim: it asserts that every subfamily of rational boxes is a basis. This stronger claim implies the informal claim (take S to be the full collection) but is provably false: e.g., S = ∅ satisfies the hypothesis trivially but is not a basis (⋃₀ ∅ ≠ univ), and S = {Ioo 0 1 × Ioo 0 1} is a single rational box but does not cover ℝ². The error is a misunderstanding of what the informal statement asks — conflating "the collection of all rational boxes" with "any collection of rational boxes."
